results.tagline: Pineapple-kissed al pastor meets WeHo's avocado obsession — bright, smoky, California-true.
ingredients: chicken al pastor (pineapple, achiote, chipotle — LTO)
corn salsa (corn, poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
guacamole (avocado, lime, cilantro, red onion, jalapeño)
results.gap_rationale: Gracias Madre and street taco culture drive al pastor demand in WeHo; locals eating it nightly on Melrose. This is Chipotle's sharpest match against that craving — al pastor is already on the LTO menu.
results.confidence_reason: All ingredients confirmed in pantry. Al pastor LTO is live. Corn salsa + guac deliver the bright, charred-sweetness profile locals expect. No pantry stretching required.

results.tagline: Chipotle-braised beef, tomatillo heat, and corn salsa crunch — the WeHo late-night taco.
ingredients: corn salsa (corn, poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
results.trend_signal: birria rising — recent_4w 52.0, avg_12m 46.6 (US-CA); barbacoa captures similar braised-beef craving authentically
results.gap_rationale: Birria demand is surging in WeHo but Chipotle can't do consommé-dipped birria (gap below). Barbacoa on crispy corn tortilla is the honest, authentic pivot that delivers deep braised beef flavor without faking the birria experience.
results.confidence_reason: Pantry fully supports this. Confidence is MID (not HIGH) because barbacoa tacos are not a new concept at Chipotle — novelty is lower. The framing against rising birria interest is the unlock.

missing_ingredients: consommé (birria dipping broth — requires guajillo, ancho, cascabel dried chiles, beef bone broth reduction)
dried guajillo chile
dried ancho chile
Oaxacan cheese (quesillo) for birria quesatacos
birria-specific chile-rubbed braising liquid separate from barbacoa prep
results.local_evidence: Gracias Madre (5333 Yelp reviews, 8905 Melrose) runs plant-based Mexican with deep mole-adjacent sauces; the WeHo street food corridor means locals know what real birria consommé tastes like. Chipotle's barbacoa alone does not replicate the dip-and-fry quesataco experience driving birria demand.
results.solution: Add a dried chile consommé sauce station (guajillo + ancho reduction) as a limited dipping broth SKU, plus Oaxacan quesillo. One sauce + one cheese = birria quesatacos unlocked.

missing_ingredients: cotija cheese (crumbled, salty, dry — Monterey Jack is a different profile entirely)
Mexican crema (sour cream is thinner and less rich)
chili powder / tajín for elote dusting
charred whole corn on cob (or fire-roasted loose corn specifically prepared elote-style)
results.local_evidence: Elote is a fixture at every WeHo food event and Melrose street food stand. Corn salsa exists in Chipotle's pantry but it's a fresh cold salsa — not the hot, creamy, charred, cotija-dusted elote experience locals are searching for at rising rates (recent_4w 20.8, up from 16.9 avg).
results.solution: Add cotija crumble as a topping SKU and a crema sauce packet. Combined with existing corn salsa and tomatillo red chili salsa, this creates an elote bowl build without infrastructure changes.

missing_ingredients: chocolate (Mexican dark chocolate — mole negro requires 30%+ of flavor from chocolate)
toasted nuts (almonds, peanuts, pepitas — structural mole thickener)
30+ dried chile varieties (ancho, mulato, pasilla, chihuacle, guajillo at minimum)
raisins or dried fruit (mole sweetness base)
Mexican cinnamon, clove, black pepper blend
plantain or stale bread (mole body)
results.local_evidence: Mole is the #1 trending Mexican flavor in CA (recent_4w 76.0, peak 100). Gracias Madre's entire identity is built on complex plant-based sauces in this family. Chipotle's tomatillo red chili salsas are distant cousins — they cannot approximate mole's chocolate-nut-chile depth.
results.solution: This is a 6–8 SKU addition minimum to do mole honestly. More realistic: partner with an established mole brand (e.g., Doña María or a premium co-packer) for a mole drizzle sauce — single SKU that unlocks the trend without building from scratch.

results.tagline: Spiced lamb, earthy beluga lentils, harissa heat — the WeHo Mediterranean bowl that earns its place next to Crossroads.
ingredients: braised lamb (garlic, onion, turmeric, chili powder)
results.trend_signal: harissa rising — recent_4w 7.2, avg_12m 7.1 (US-CA); lentils rising — recent_4w 47.0, avg_12m 46.7
results.gap_rationale: Crossroads Kitchen (3504 Yelp reviews, 8284 Melrose) serves vegan Mediterranean to a WeHo crowd hungry for complex plant-forward flavors. CAVA's braised lamb + lentil base + harissa + sumac slaw is the fast-casual answer — layered, spiced, and authentically in-pantry.
results.confidence_reason: Every element confirmed in CAVA pantry. Harissa is a native CAVA ingredient. Beluga lentils are a menu base. Braised lamb is a protein option. Sumac slaw is a listed topping. No stretching.

results.tagline: Whipped jalapeño feta, charred vegetables, fire-roasted corn — CAVA's answer to WeHo's plant-forward obsession.
results.trend_signal: cauliflower rising — recent_4w 69.0 (US-CA); lentils rising — recent_4w 47.0
results.gap_rationale: Open Sesame (1042 reviews, 7458 Beverly) and Crossroads show WeHo diners are comfortable with bold vegetable-forward builds. Crazy feta provides a spicy, creamy anchor that elevates CAVA's veggie plate above generic salad territory.
results.confidence_reason: All confirmed pantry. Crazy feta is a signature CAVA item. Fire-roasted corn is listed. Roasted veg is a protein option. This is a zero-gap dish build.

missing_ingredients: gochujang (fermented Korean chile paste — base of all Korean heat flavor)
sesame oil (toasted — mandatory Korean aromatic)
doenjang or miso (Korean/Japanese fermented soybean base)
kimchi (fermented napa cabbage — distinct from any CAVA pickle)
scallion / ginger as primary aromatics (not herbs)
results.local_evidence: Moonbowls (8320 W 3rd, 170 reviews but cult-dense) is doing Korean grain bowls directly in CAVA's format lane. Kimchi trending at recent_4w 54.8 in CA — the highest Korean signal in the market. CAVA's sesame citrus herb dressing contains sesame flavor notes but not the fermented heat backbone that defines gochujang-Korean food.
results.solution: Add gochujang as a dip/spread SKU (parallels harissa structurally — both are chile pastes). Single SKU addition would immediately unlock Korean bowl builds using existing rice bases, roasted veg, and sesame dressing.

missing_ingredients: eggs (poached or baked — shakshuka is defined by eggs cooked in tomato-pepper sauce)
cumin + paprika in tomato sauce format (distinct from current tomato + onion topping)
bell pepper braised in spiced tomato (structured as sauce, not fresh topping)
results.local_evidence: Shakshuka is a rising search term (recent_4w 7.0, trend: rising) in CA. K'Far in Williamsburg proves the Israeli brunch format translates to fast-casual desire. In WeHo, Israeli-Mediterranean food drives traffic at multiple price points. CAVA's tomato + onion topping and harissa exist but there is no egg protein and no sauce-base format to build a true shakshuka.
results.solution: Add a soft-cooked egg as a protein option (one SKU). Combined with harissa, tomato + onion, feta, and arugula — shakshuka bowl is unlocked without a sauce addition.

results.tagline: Miso-glazed salmon, yuzu-adjacent citrus brightness, sesame and nori — the WeHo umami bowl Moonbowls can't wrap.
results.trend_signal: yuzu rising — recent_4w 31.0, avg_12m 28.4 (US-CA); kimchi rising — recent_4w 54.8
results.gap_rationale: Moonbowls (Korean bowls, 8320 W 3rd) and Japanese-adjacent dining in WeHo show strong umami-forward demand. Sweetgreen's Miso Glazed Salmon is already on the permanent menu — this frames it as the WeHo local response to the yuzu/umami trend wave, leaning into nori sesame seasoning as the trend-aligned element.
results.confidence_reason: This is a confirmed existing Sweetgreen dish (Miso Glazed Salmon). All ingredients verified in sweetgreen_menu.json. Framing against local Korean bowl competition and rising yuzu signal is the LTO pitch angle, not a new build.

results.tagline: Apple kimchi sauce, KBBQ dressing, crispy onions — WeHo's Korean fusion craving, wrapped and ready.
results.trend_signal: kimchi rising — recent_4w 54.8, avg_12m 53.4 (US-CA); bulgogi rising — recent_4w 22.5
results.gap_rationale: Moonbowls is the direct local competitor here. Sweetgreen has a confirmed KBBQ wrap (Saucy KBBQ Chicken) with apple kimchi sauce and KBBQ dressing — this is their sharpest Korean-adjacent offering and it's already built. The WeHo opportunity is to lean into it aggressively given Moonbowls' limited scale (170 reviews).
results.confidence_reason: Confirmed existing dish in sweetgreen_menu.json. Apple kimchi sauce and KBBQ dressing are verified pantry items. WeHo's Korean food culture (Moonbowls) validates the demand. No stretching.

missing_ingredients: cotija cheese (crumbled — parmesan is not a substitute)
Mexican crema (no equivalent in Sweetgreen pantry)
tajín or chili-lime seasoning
fire-roasted / charred corn (corn salsa exists in one wrap but not as a standalone elote preparation)
results.local_evidence: Elote is surging in WeHo (recent_4w 20.8, up from 16.9 avg). Gracias Madre and local taco stands set the expectation for authentic cotija + crema presentation. Sweetgreen has no cotija, no crema, and no chili-lime seasoning — three of the four defining elote elements are missing.
results.solution: Add cotija crumble as a topping SKU. This single addition, paired with corn salsa (already in the Jalapeño Ranch wrap), lime squeeze, and charred jalapeño ranch, creates a credible elote bowl without a full rebuild.

missing_ingredients: Mexican dark chocolate
dried chile blend (ancho, guajillo, pasilla minimum)
toasted nuts (pepitas, almonds)
mole sauce base — no approximation exists in Sweetgreen pantry
results.local_evidence: Mole is the #1 trending Mexican flavor in CA (recent_4w 76.0). Gracias Madre's plant-based Mexican identity in WeHo makes this a neighborhood-level flavor expectation. Sweetgreen has no Mexican flavor infrastructure at all — no adobo, no chipotle, no dried chiles, no chocolate.
results.solution: This requires a dedicated mole-inspired sauce SKU (licensed or co-developed). Sweetgreen's existing proteins (roasted chicken, blackened chicken) would be the carriers. Without the sauce, the trend is completely unreachable.
